description_long: This object is a cylindrical stone with intricate carvings along its length, featuring raised and recessed patterns that suggest stylized motifs or figures. The stone is hollow, as seen from the open ends, and the interior appears roughly finished with visible tool marks. The exterior surface is predominantly matte and shows signs of wear, with softened edges and some minor chipping. The carvings include repeating bands and possibly abstract or zoomorphic forms, giving the object a decorative and possibly symbolic character. The stone is a pale, off-white color, typical of weathered limestone or marble, and there are no visible traces of paint or additional materials. The overall craftsmanship and weathering suggest it may have served as an architectural or ornamental element in an urban setting.
description: A carved, cylindrical pale stone with intricate raised patterns and hollow interior, showing signs of wear and age.
description_view_0: Side view showing the detailed carvings and the cylindrical shape, with the raised motifs clearly visible.
description_view_1: End-on view revealing the hollow interior and the thickness of the stone walls, with some markings visible on the rim.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting different carved patterns and the continuity of the decorative band around the cylinder.
description_view_3: End-on view from the other side, showing the hollow core and the rough, unfinished look of the interior.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or decorative element on the ground
attribute_pattern: Intricate carved patterns with repeating bands and stylized motifs, irregular but intentional in design.
attribute_weathering: Edges are softened and surface is matte, indicating moderate weathering and age.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with modern urban materials but may blend with other stone features or landscaping elements.
